session.assistant: Now let me run the tests to verify they pass:

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run pytest tests/commands/pr/test_list.py -x -v and report results. Focus on the two new tests: test_pr_list_excludes_learn_plans_by_default and test_pr_list_includes_learn_plans_with_flag.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Here's my summary:
## Test Results
pytest passed: All 14 tests in test_list.py passed in 2.24s
### Focus Tests - Both Passing
**1. `test_pr_list_excludes_learn_plans_by_default` (line 738)** - Creates two plans: one regular plan (#1) and one learn plan (#2, with "erk-learn" label) - Runs `erk pr list` without flags - Asserts: - Exit code is 0 (success) - Output shows "Found 1 plan(s)" - Regular plan #1 is displayed - Learn plan #2 is NOT displayed (excluded by default)
**2. `test_pr_list_includes_learn_plans_with_flag` (line 788)** - Creates the same two plans - Runs `erk pr list --include-learn` - Asserts: - Exit code is 0 (success) - Output shows "Found 2 plan(s)" - Both regular plan #1 and learn plan #2 are displayed
### Key Findings
Both tests are passing, confirming that: - Learn plans (identified by the "erk-learn" label) are excluded from `erk pr list` by default - The `--include-learn` flag allows learn plans to be displayed alongside regular plans
... [4 lines omitted] ...
